name: data_lineage_provenance_v1
description: adapterOS data lineage and provenance training data: forward/backward lineage tracing through the full chain (Source Documents → Ingestion Pipeline → Dataset → Training Run → Adapter Bundle → Deployment → Routing Decisions → Inference Results), impact analysis for source document changes, compliance verification of training data provenance, and lineage graph operations including orphan detection and garbage collection.

entries.notes: 2000 examples across five categories: forward_lineage (400), backward_lineage (400), impact_analysis (400), compliance_verification (400), lineage_operations (400). Every example uses adapterOS canonical types (DataLineageMode, DatasetVersionSelection, TrainingVersioningContext, AdapterTrainingSnapshot), real ID prefixes, BLAKE3 hashes, and the actual provenance chain as implemented in adapteros-orchestrator, adapteros-db, and adapteros-types.

provenance.review_notes: All lineage chain nodes, ID prefixes, hash algorithms, trust states, and provenance metadata sourced directly from the adapterOS codebase. DataLineageMode variants (Versioned, DatasetOnly, Synthetic, LegacyUnpinned), BLAKE3 hashing via blake3::Hasher, compute_combined_data_spec_hash deterministic sort, AdapterTrainingSnapshot schema, and TrainingReportV1 fields all match production types. Trust state normalization (allowed, needs_approval, blocked_regressed) follows canonical_trust_state from adapteros-api-types.

evaluation_gates: lineage chain references use correct adapterOS ID prefixes (dst-, dsv-, run-, adp-, col-, ten-)
all content integrity references use BLAKE3 (64-char hex strings), never SHA-256 or MD5
DataLineageMode is exactly one of: versioned, dataset_only, synthetic, legacy_unpinned
trust_state values are canonical: allowed, needs_approval, blocked_regressed, unknown
forward lineage correctly traces source → dataset → training_run → adapter → deployment
backward lineage correctly traces adapter → training_run → dataset → source_documents
impact analysis identifies all downstream artifacts when a source node changes
compliance verification checks both trust_state and hash integrity at every chain link
lineage graph operations use correct query patterns (forward traversal, backward traversal, reachability)
provenance metadata matches AdapterTrainingSnapshot schema fields exactly
intent: Teach adapters to trace the full provenance chain in adapterOS: query forward and backward lineage across the Source → Dataset → TrainingRun → Adapter → Deployment graph, perform impact analysis when source documents change, verify compliance by proving training data meets trust requirements with hash integrity at every link, and operate on the lineage graph to detect orphans, garbage-collect unreferenced artifacts, and visualize dependency trees.

subcategories.forward_lineage: Given a source document or dataset, trace forward through the lineage graph to find all derived training runs, adapter bundles, deployments, and inference results. Each hop includes the node ID, BLAKE3 hash, timestamp, and actor.
subcategories.backward_lineage: Given a deployed adapter or inference result, trace backward through the lineage graph to the original source documents. Verify hash integrity at each link. Reconstruct the full provenance chain with all intermediate nodes.
subcategories.impact_analysis: Given a change to a source document, dataset, or base model, determine which downstream artifacts are affected. Identify which adapters need retraining, which deployments need updating, and estimate blast radius across tenants and routing rules.
subcategories.compliance_verification: Prove that an adapter was trained exclusively on approved data sources. Verify trust_state at every dataset version, confirm no unauthorized data sources contributed, validate BLAKE3 hash chain integrity, and generate audit-ready compliance reports.
subcategories.lineage_operations: Query the lineage graph using structured operations: find orphaned artifacts with no upstream references, detect unreferenced datasets eligible for garbage collection, compute dependency depth, visualize lineage trees, and merge lineage graphs across tenants.
